interactions.body:
(Borrower CreditBuilder - CANCEL Email) [RE:Welcome to Together Loans Credit Builder!] Hi Hilda,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that on 03/25/2026 you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $7000 loan. I can confirm that I have canceled your Together Loans CreditBuilder, you will not be charged again.

The Together Loans CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Together Loans can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Together Loans CreditBuilder here - https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 700+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. 

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
James, Together Loans

(AI CB ORANGE - Already cancelled Email) [RE: Welcome to Together Loans Credit Builder!] Hi Hilda,

Thanks for getting in touch.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $10,000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

I can see that you signed up for a Together Loans CreditBuilder account when applying for a loan of $7000, on 03/25/2026. We sent you a ‘Welcome to Credit Builder' email on sign-up, confirming your account, how it works and how you can manage it.

Your CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

However, I can confirm that your CreditBuilder account has been canceled, so no further payments will be taken. If you wish to set-up a new CreditBuilder account, you can do so here https://tcrdt.com/s/HPPU9QY2. CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/

Together Loans can still help you access funds of up to $10,000 with the help of a cosigner. You can always access your application here: https://tcrdt.com/s/0P5GBAT9

If you need help, please let me know.

Many thanks,
Ben, Together Loans

Thanks for choosing Transform Credit, we have attached your Proposed Credit Contract with this email for you to keep. This does not mean that your application has been approved, but it gives you a chance to read and understand the terms of the agreement. 

To finalize your application please complete all of the required steps online - https://transformcredit.com/hub?aref=*****************63416 

If you have any questions about our application process please visit our website https://transformcredit.com/faq

Amber @ Transform Credit

[Affordability Confirmation] To KATHERINE ******,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to charges of criminal fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

Information provided to Transform Credit by you:

Borrower: KATHERINE ******
IP: 76.39.105.140
DateTime (UTC): 11-30-2023
Stated Monthly Income: Full Time Employed $1150
Rent: $200
Bills: $0
Other Expenses: $120
Your Credit Commitments: $160
Transform Credit Repayment: $191.870000
To be safe: $50
Total available after expenditure and this loan repayment: $428.130000


Yours sincerely
Transform credit Applications Dept

interactions.body:
(Borrower GT Default Email) [RE:We'll keep your $3000 reserved for another 20 days] Hi Mary, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 750
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/UD8TGQ0A

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit

Hi Mary, thanks for contacting Transform Credit. We can help you through this channel or by email. Please reply to this message with your question or email hello@transformcredit.com, quoting *****************42300, and we will answer as soon as possible. Thanks, Transform Credit

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Default SMS) Hi ALFRED, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tglns.com/s/1YEULIAO - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Together Loans

(Borrower GT Default Email) [RE:REG : LOAN APPLIED] Hi Jennifer, 

Here at Together Loans, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 700
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://togetherloans.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/EM1K9PRH

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with CANCEL. You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://togetherloans.com

Thanks,
Together Loans

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Joshua, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/0BP9IIOA - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

interactions.label: Message from MessageFactoryBot — INVITE GT BRW Email DSIT1

interactions.body: (AI BW GTQs - Don't have/won't provide GT SMS) Hi Joshua, Together Loans is a cosigner-only lender, which means all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. If you can't provide a suitable cosigner, we can cancel your application if you prefer. You can apply again anytime! You can reply CANCEL to this message, or use this link to continue when you are ready: https://tcrdt.com/s/JIVKLDEU Thanks, Together Loans

interactions.body: (AI BW GTQs - Can I get loan without GT SMS) Hi Stacy, based on your situation, we require you to provide a cosigner. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you to pay, and that we should too! This allows us to lend to you even if you've been turned down elsewhere. If you could get a loan elsewhere without a cosigner, we might not be the right choice for you. If you have a suitable cosigner send them this link, they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/LJKA13AU Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Together Loans

I have now cancelled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. You can find out more about the benefits of CreditBuilder here: https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/. You can find out more about the benefits of CreditBuilder here: https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/. In the meantime, the loan for $5000 is still approved, click here to pick up where you left off https://tcrdt.com/s/KJ6FTOAD. Jade, Together Loans

(Borrower CreditBuilder - CANCEL Email) [RE:Cancel application] Hi Manuel,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that on 08/12/2025 you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $5000 loan. I can confirm that I have canceled your Together Loans CreditBuilder, you will not be charged again.

The Together Loans CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Together Loans can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Together Loans CreditBuilder here - https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 700+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. 

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
James, Together Loans

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Default SMS) Hi CHELZIE, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/S46EAY55 - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. Hi Rena,

Thank you for reaching out.

New account will take some time to get registered in the system. And, we will be unable to verify the same until it gets registered in the system.

We use an online verification system, and the account is verified in this system when it is entered. However, new accounts take time to be registered in this system so that we can verify them. The amount of time taken will depend on the individual bank.

This is the reason we request all our customers to add a longstanding checking account that is active, valid, in their name, having a positive balance and capable of ACH payments.

Transform Credit

interactions.body:
(Appeals Auto Response GT Email) [RE:Hi] Hi , 

Thanks for contacting Appeals. 

We understand that it may be disheartening or concerning if you've been considered an unsuitable cosigner, don't worry though - the most important thing is that we can still help the borrower.

There are a few reasons we could find someone to be unsuitable - the most common being a less than perfect credit history. Being a cosigner is a big financial commitment, they are responsible for making any loan payments that their borrower does not. Reviewing all cosigners' Experian credit histories allows us to check that they would be able to manage any payments they are required to make, in addition to maintaining any other existing commitments. If a cosigner has had missed or late payments in the past, we can't be certain that being a cosigner s a good idea and wouldn't see them struggle to keep their other accounts up to date. Alternatively, If a cosigner's credit file is locked when they apply, we won't have been able to confirm either way (if this has happened with you, you can reapply as soon as your credit file is unlocked: https://tcrdt.com/s/CTLZO0Q1). 

A cosigner must have a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners and mortgage payers are more likely to be accepted). It's important to remember that a cosigner can only be on one TransformCredit loan at a time too. If you'd like to review the information that we used in our assessment of your credit, you can check your Experian credit report/score for free at: https://www.experian.com/. 

In the meantime, the $0 will be reserved for the borrower. We'd love for their loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like them. If you have any other questions, you may find the answer here: https://transformcredit.com/faq.

Jade@TransformCredit

interactions.body:
[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : *****************13423] Hi Lisa,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $7000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly interest payments against, building up their credit score.

I can see that you signed up for a Transform CreditBuilder account when applied for a loan of $3000. The transform CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Transform Credit can still help you access funds of up to $7000 too! At which point, the CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ but I can confirm that I have canceled this now for you, in line with your request. 

Unfortunately, I will not be able to issue a refund or reverse the payments as you opted into CreditBuilder while applying on our website, however, I can confirm that the CreditBuilder is already canceled, and will show as a settled loan on your credit file, which will have a positive impact on your credit score. Please allow up to 45 days to have this updated.

Regards,
Credit Builder Team

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Lisa, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/LLQ0DSA8 - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Transform Credit

interactions.body:
[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : *****************22620] Hi Eric,
 
Thank you for getting in touch.
 
We can see that you signed up for a CreditBuilder Account when applied for a loan of $6000. We confirm that your Transform Credit Builder Account is cancelled, and you will not be charged again.

The CreditBuilder Account helps you to build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Transform Credit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Transform Credit Builder Account here: https://transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/

We also confirm that your loan application for $6000 is cancelled. If you decide to apply again, you can use this link: https://rgct.io/s/SON27N4N

If you need help, please let us know.
 
Have a great day, 
Michelle@TransformCredit

interactions.body:
(CB CANCEL - Specific email subject lines) [RE:Welcome to Transform Credit Builder!] Hi Fanny,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $3000 loan on 06/28/2025. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as payment for a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, your CreditBuilder account is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, TransformCredit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Transform CreditBuilder here - transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ . However, I can confirm that I have canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. 

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 750+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. Follow this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/SFKGICXJ

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
Jade @ Transform CreditBuilder

interactions.body:
(Product Questions BRW Email) [RE:Emma needs to finalize their agreement here: https://tcrdt.com/s/LZUB1EIZ] Hi Fanny

Here at Transform Credit, we're a lender but our loans are a little different. Most lenders look at your credit history to decide whether you can borrow from them, based on how you've previously handled your money. We don't do that - our loans are based on the trust of your cosigner. 

We offer loans between $3,000 and $10,000 over 24 - 60 months, in an ever-growing number of states. The loan amounts and rates we offer are state-dependent - you can find our state license details here: https://transformcredit.com/Licenses. Your instalment payments will depend on your chosen loan (you can check the options using our sliders at: https://transformcredit.com). Our maximum APR is 35.99%, with no hidden fees! The interest on your loan is calculated daily, so if you pay your loan back early it'll cost you less in interest. 

If you'd like to check, or change the specific payment amounts of your loan, you can here: https://tcrdt.com/s/1ZO5IV56

If you'd like to read more about what our existing customers think about our service, you can check out our reviews at: https://ie.trustpilot.com/review/transformcredit.com. You may also find answers to your other questions on our website in our frequently asked questions section: https://transformcredit.com/Help/Contact. Otherwise, you're welcome to reply to this email explaining in detail and someone will get back to you as soon as possible. 

Thanks,
Jade@TransformCredit

spk_1: Tatiana Calling from Together. Thanks,
spk_0: Tatiana. Please stay on the line.

Hi Sonya, thank you for reaching out. I understand you would like to change your due date going forward, however I would like to advise you that at this point in time, since your next installment is due today, we will not be able to change your due date until the account is brought current again. I do see there is a secondary promise to pay in place, please keep in mind that with this payment arrangement it only stops collections from you, it does not stop collections from your co-signor. The reason being is since the account is still so fairly new and the balance is at the second highest it can possibly be, the interest that will be accrued during this time frame will make the loan more expensive in the long run, therefore increasing both yours and your co-signor's liability in the loan. Please note, you can change your due date going forward by clicking this link https://tcrdt.com/s/6GY5NH6P once the account is current, please ensure to do so at least 2 business days before your next payment is due. Thank you, Together Loans

spk_1: Hi, this message is for Samuel ****** Junior. Samuel, this is Tyler Calling with Together Loans. We just spoke and um we were disconnected. I'm Calling because the arrangement, the secondary promise to pay that Sonia made is not a form. Promised to pay and it was stated to her when she was setting it up. The date that she chose puts the account at high risk of the full balance being Due. Um, the loan will go into Default and the full balance will become Due. As a co-signer, you did agree to be equally responsible for this loan and the Repayment. So per our separate contract with you, we are expecting you to step in and make a Payment if she is Unable to. Our phone number is ******* option two. Thank you.

Hi Samuel, Sonya has a secondary promise to pay and should not be considered a formal payment plan. This was clearly stated to Sonya when setting it up and in the email sent afterwards, that it would not stop us from collecting from you. Due to the proposed date, the account is at high risk of owing the full balance. Therefore, per your separate agreement, we expect you to make a payment of $172.41 at https://tcrdt.com/s/8NDGH54O to avoid the full balance of the loan becoming due. Best regards, Tyla@Together Loans

interactions.body: [Your Transform Credit Loan Application Ref: *****************40509] I don’t have a co-signer

(Borrower GT Default Email) [RE:Your Transform Credit Loan Application Ref: *****************40509] Hi Dean, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 750
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/0SUFLWCI

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit

[Your Transform Credit Loan Application Ref: *****************10030] Hello Daniel,

Thank you for starting your Transform Credit loan application. 

Below is a link where you can view/download our free Credit Education Program. You're not required to do anything with this in order to proceed with your application, but we recommend reviewing the content as you may find it helpful.

https://transformcredit.com/credit-education-program?state=ca

Transform Credit Inc.

[Affordability Confirmation] To Daniel ******,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

Information provided to Transform Credit by you:

Borrower: Daniel ******
IP: 104.28.116.26
DateTime (UTC): 01-11-2025
Stated Monthly Income: Full Time Employed $4000
Rent: $1800
Bills: $500
Other Expenses: $300
Your Credit Commitments: $250
Transform Credit Repayment: $177.130000
To be safe: $50
Total available after expenditure and this loan repayment: $922.870000


Yours sincerely
Transform credit Applications Dept

Hi Latrice. Thanks for reaching out to Transform Credit, but I think you sent us a message that was not intended for us.

To continue your application, you will need a cosigner. Please, use this link to invite them: https://tcrdt.com/s/5Q67ZDE1  
You might find this page helpful: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans - you could also send it to a potential cosigner to help answer any of their questions.

We aim to pay out every loan within 24 hours of the cosigner being accepted. If you and your cosigner complete the application online and all checks are completed without issue, we could get the money to you sooner! If you need any help, just let me know. 
Thanks, Africa@TransformCredit

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 060277CARD] Dear Kerry and Tibor,

In reference to our letter dated February 6th, please find attached a copy of Latrice’s contract as the original Creditor as confirmation that this is her loan.

Per your request, we have now ceased all direct communication with the client. 

Please note that this is a dual-liability loan with a co-signer. The monthly payment of $177.13 is due on the 1st of each month. At the moment, the loan is current, and the next payment is due on March 1st. If payment is not received in full and on time from Latrice, we will proceed with contacting and collecting from the co-signer under their separate agreement to make any to all payments Latrice fails. This is to prevent the full balance from becoming due and to avoid the risk of legal escalation against both parties. Additionally, to prevent a negative impact on their credit file, if the loan defaults, we will report this loan as delinquent 

Best,
Berginie
Head of Collections
Transform Credit

interactions.body:
(AI BW Bank - Bank criteria Email) [RE: A message from Transform Credit] Hi BEN,

We accept most banks and credit unions.

However, we do not accept online banks such as Chime and Cashapp, as we cannot verify the cosigner's identity through these companies. We also can't accept joint accounts between borrowers and cosigners, or prepaid accounts. Robert must provide an account that is open, valid, in their name and capable of ACH payments.

In order to proceed, please ask Robert to add a suitable bank here so we can complete your loan: https://tcrdt.com/s/RGDICYMP

Thanks, Transform Credit

(Appeals Auto Response BRW Email) [RE:Cosigner on Loan] Hi BEN, 

Thanks for contacting Appeals. 

We understand that it can be disheartening when you're struggling to find a suitable cosigner, don't worry though - the most important thing is we've already accepted your application and want to help you.

There are a few reasons we could find someone to be unsuitable - the most common being a less than perfect credit history. It will be up to your cosigner to make any loan payments that you do not, looking at their Experian credit history allows us to check that they can manage this in addition to their existing commitments. If a potential cosigner has missed or late payments, we can't be certain they'd be able to afford the loan payments while maintaining their other accounts. Alternatively, If your potential cosigner's credit file is locked, we won't have been able to confirm either way (they can apply as soon as their credit file is unlocked: https://tcrdt.com/s/S5779DGZ). 

When you're considering who to ask, remember your cosigner must have a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners and mortgage payers are more likely to be accepted). It's important to remember that your cosigner can only be on one TransformCredit loan at a time too.

If you've provided someone who is unsure why they weren't accepted, we recommend they check their Experian credit report/score for free at: https://www.experian.com/.

In the meantime, the $4500 will still be reserved for you. We'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. Give this link to your cosigner to register online: https://tcrdt.com/s/S5779DGZ and feel free to check out our FAQs for further help: https://transformcredit.com/faq.

Jade@TransformCredit 

P.S. I am ready to pay $4500 out within 24 hours of your cosigner being approved, but if you don't plan to provide a new cosigner please let me know by replying CANCEL so I can reserve it for someone else

Hi BEN. Your Credit File is locked. To continue with your application, you must unlock it by contacting Experian, visiting their website or using the Experian App. Once done, reply 'UNLOCKED' to this message and we will process your application for you. Thanks, Ben at Transform Credit

interactions.body: Hi Joseorlando. Thank you for reaching out. We understand this situation has been disappointing. While checking, I could confirm that, unfortunately, your previous cosigner has failed our final checks and therefore is not suitable for your loan. Due to data protection, I can't share any specific reason, we have sent them an email explaining the reason behind this decision. Thank you for contacting Transform Credit. However, I can confirm that at this time, our infrastructure only supports being able to lend to those that can understand English - our contracts, website, communications, and staff are not set up to support other languages. Being a borrower or a cosigner is a serious responsibility and it would be unlawful for us to lend to someone where we knew there was an obvious language barrier, putting that person into a vulnerable situation where they may not fully understand the agreement they are entering into.  We appreciate your understanding. We can still pay out your $5000 once you find a suitable cosigner. Remember, a suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 700 and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner. Just use give your new cosigner this link to apply online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/LVCWRUEQ Thanks, Transform Credit

(Borrower GT Default Email) [RE:Last day for FastTrack] Hi ALEXIS, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 750
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/6JKK5FBG

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit
